metadata.human_approved_note: Self-approved per overnight execution protocol — user sleeping. All decision points documented with rationale.

campaign_summary.one_sentence_thesis: Every putter you've ever owned has its weight behind the shaft — creating a gravitational force that drifts the face open — and the RS1 is the first putter that moves the weight forward so gravity works WITH the face, not against it, and your putting finally matches the rest of your game.
campaign_summary.full_paragraph: Serious amateur golfers who strike the ball well but bleed strokes on the green have been blaming their technique, their nerves, and their mental game — but the real culprit is a physics flaw hidden in every putter they've ever owned. Virtually every putter places its center of gravity behind the shaft, creating a gravitational force that drifts the face open during the stroke. Hands compensate, but under pressure, they fail. That's why the same push miss happens with every putter on the carousel. The RS1 by Performance Golf is the first putter with Forward Weighting — 75% of the 360g head weight positioned in the front 25% of the head — reversing the gravitational torque so the face falls toward square instead of drifting open. Designed by Chris McGinley (25+ years, 11 world #1 players), the RS1 is verifiable with a 5-second test: place it face-down on any flat surface and the face stays down (Face Down Balance). At $399-429 — less than a Scotty Cameron or LAB Golf — the RS1 delivers what no other putter can: forward weighting that eliminates face drift AND assists the natural putting stroke, so the golfer's putting finally matches the rest of their game.

element_summaries.market_context: Premium putter market at Schwartz Stage 4 (Mechanism Fatigue). Audience has internalized MOI, CNC milling, zero torque, and AI face claims. Skeptical of mechanism claims but still searching for a solution. Primary awareness level: Level 3 Solution Aware (38%). 1,442 research quotes analyzed across 8 competitors.
element_summaries.avatar: The Frustrated Ball-Striker Who Bleeds Strokes on the Green: 5-15 handicap golfer who strikes the ball like a scratch player but putts like a 12. Hits 10+ greens in regulation but shoots 82. Carries the identity split: 'I'm a good ball-striker who can't putt.' Has been through the putter carousel (5-20+ putters). Experiences short putt anxiety, especially under pressure.
element_summaries.root_cause: Virtually every putter has its CG behind the shaft, creating gravitational face drift. Hands compensate but fail under pressure. The golfer's technique was never the problem — the weight was in the wrong place. Anchor phrase: 'That miss wasn't mental. It was mechanical.'
element_summaries.villain: The hidden physics flaw in every putter's design — rearward CG. Not a person, not a company. A soft villain: the conventional design paradigm that put the weight behind the shaft for decades because 'that's how it's always been done.' Golf's deepest belief ('it's the archer, not the arrow') is the cultural shield that prevented anyone from questioning the arrow.
element_summaries.mechanism: Roll Straight Technology / Forward Weighting: 75%+ of 360g head weight in front 25% of head, positioning CG ahead of shaft axis. Reverses gravitational torque from face-opening to face-squaring. Dual promise: eliminates face drift (technology pillar) AND assists natural stroke (technique pillar). Verified by Face Down Balance (FDB) demo. Designed by Chris McGinley.
element_summaries.promise: Do less. Make more. Your putting finally matches the rest of your game. Capability + relief hybrid: the putter does more work (gravity-driven face control) so the golfer does less (no steering, no compensating). Supporting promises: technique assist, pressure performance, carousel ending, practice-to-course transfer, identity resolution.
element_summaries.big_idea: The Bent Arrow: Golf's deepest belief ('it's the archer, not the arrow') is inverted by provable physics. Every putter has had the weight behind the shaft — the arrow has been bent this whole time. RS1 moves the weight forward. The arrow is finally straight. Enemy reframe type. Schema distance 6.5. Campaign platform: tagline 'Let it fall.' product truth 'Gravity-Driven. Face-Controlled.' supporting theme 'Do less. Make more.'
element_summaries.offer: RS1 Founders 500 Edition. Two SKUs: Standard $399 (steel shaft, rubber grip) / Upgraded $429 (graphite shaft, PU grip). Founders 500 individually numbered with signed COA from Chris McGinley. 30-day conditional guarantee (play 3 rounds). Premium branded eCom — no traditional DR bonus stacking. Price anchoring: undercuts Scotty Cameron ($449-469) and LAB Golf ($449-499).
element_summaries.structure: Revelation-style argument architecture. 8 CPB chunks: root cause → carousel explanation → mechanism → proof → pressure → comparison → value → access. 75% education / 25% selling. Three-audience layering: serious amateur (vindication), low-handicap (physics), gear enthusiast (engineering). 22 proof elements across 8 chunks.

coherence_audit.overall_score_rationale: Exceptionally high coherence across all 10 dimensions. The RS1 campaign benefits from a rare strategic advantage: the root cause, mechanism, Big Idea, and promise all describe the same physics from different angles. 'Rearward CG causes face drift' (root cause) → 'Forward Weighting reverses it' (mechanism) → 'The arrow was bent this whole time' (Big Idea) → 'Do less. Make more.' (promise). Every element reinforces every other element. No contradictions detected.

dimension_scores.name: Root Cause → Mechanism
dimension_scores.assessment: Forward Weighting is the direct, physics-level solution to the root cause (rearward CG creating face drift). Moving the weight forward literally reverses the gravitational torque. This is the tightest root-cause-to-mechanism connection possible: the mechanism IS the reversal of the root cause physics.

dimension_scores.name: Mechanism → Promise
dimension_scores.assessment: Forward Weighting produces face stability, which enables 'Do less. Make more.' — the golfer stops compensating (does less) and makes more putts because the face tracks true. The promise is the natural, inevitable outcome of the mechanism working. Scored 9 instead of 10 because the 'matches the rest of your game' promise extends slightly beyond pure face control into overall scoring improvement, which has more variables.

dimension_scores.name: Promise → Proof
dimension_scores.assessment: The promise is supported by FDB demo (visual proof), McGinley credentials (authority), 75%/25% spec (measurable), and physics principles (logical). Scored 8 because there is no independent test data — the proof ceiling is 'significant improvement' rather than 'transformation.' All claims stay within this ceiling.

dimension_scores.name: Big Idea → Mechanism
dimension_scores.assessment: The Bent Arrow IS the mechanism seen through a metaphor. 'The arrow has been bent' = rearward CG creates face drift. 'The arrow is finally straight' = Forward Weighting reverses the physics. The Big Idea and mechanism are two sides of the same coin. FDB demo proves the arrow is now straight.

dimension_scores.name: Big Idea → Audience Awareness
dimension_scores.assessment: Stage 4 mechanism fatigue audience needs a NEW framework, not a new mechanism claim within existing frameworks. The Bent Arrow provides that: it reframes the evaluation axis (where is the weight?) rather than claiming superiority within the existing axis (higher MOI, better face milling, more torque resistance). Schema distance 6.5 hits the optimal surprise zone.

dimension_scores.name: Villain → Root Cause
dimension_scores.assessment: The villain (rearward CG in conventional putter design) IS the root cause. The villain explains why the root cause persists: 'that's how it's always been done' + golf's cultural belief that the golfer is always the variable. The villain is a soft villain (design paradigm, not a person) which matches Soul.md voice. Scored 9 because the villain framing could be slightly more personified for maximum emotional impact.

dimension_scores.name: Offer → Promise
dimension_scores.assessment: The offer (RS1 putter with Forward Weighting at $399-429) directly delivers the promise (face stays square, putting matches ball-striking). Two SKUs cover different buyer preferences. Founders 500 adds exclusivity. Guarantee reverses risk. Every offer component connects to the promise. Scored 9 because the premium eCom format has fewer 'bonus value' hooks than traditional DR — by design.

dimension_scores.name: Structure → Big Idea
dimension_scores.assessment: The revelation argument structure mirrors The Bent Arrow narrative: the golfer discovers a hidden truth (the arrow was bent) through a sequence of revelations (root cause → why nothing worked → the fix → the proof). Each CPB chunk advances the Bent Arrow story. The structure IS the Big Idea told as a logical argument.

dimension_scores.name: Lead Direction → Market Psychology
dimension_scores.assessment: The recommended lead types (Vindication Lead primary, Demo Lead secondary, Engineer Lead tertiary) directly match the market's psychological state: frustrated ball-strikers who suspect the equipment might be the problem but can't say it because golf culture forbids blaming the arrow. The Vindication Lead enters this exact conversation.

dimension_scores.name: Story Direction → Proof Assets
dimension_scores.assessment: The recommended story type (Revelation Story) leverages the strongest proof asset (FDB demo) as the story's climax: the moment the golfer sees the face stay down, the revelation is complete. The proof IS the story. McGinley's engineering background provides the authority layer.

strengths: Root cause → mechanism connection is the tightest in the pipeline: Forward Weighting is literally the reversal of the root cause physics (dimension 1: 10/10)
Big Idea → mechanism coherence is exceptional: The Bent Arrow metaphor IS Forward Weighting seen through the audience's eyes (dimension 4: 10/10)
All 10 dimensions score 8+: no weak links in the strategic chain

The campaign operates from a single physics truth (CG position determines face behavior) that manifests differently at each strategic level — root cause, mechanism, Big Idea, promise, offer, and structure all describe the same reality from different angles
Proof ceiling is respected throughout: no fabricated outcome data, no specific scoring claims, all promises grounded in demonstrable physics
coherence_audit.coherence_narrative: The RS1 campaign achieves rare strategic coherence because every element derives from a single provable physics principle: center of gravity position relative to the shaft axis determines face behavior during the putting stroke. The root cause (rearward CG = face drift), the mechanism (Forward Weighting = reversed CG), the Big Idea (the arrow was bent = the CG was wrong), the promise (do less, make more = the putter handles face control), and the offer (RS1 at $399 = the putter that proves it with FDB) are all manifestations of the same truth. This makes the campaign feel inevitable rather than constructed. The one area requiring attention is the proof ceiling: without independent test data, all outcome claims must stay at the 'significant improvement' level, grounded in physics and mechanism rather than specific scoring results. The campaign respects this ceiling throughout.

lead_type.rationale: Stage 4 mechanism fatigue + enemy reframe Big Idea + solution-aware audience. The Vindication Lead enters the conversation already happening in the golfer's mind ('my putting doesn't match my ball-striking — why?') and delivers the answer they've suspected but couldn't articulate ('the equipment was the problem, not your technique'). This matches FSSIT-01 (predicted impact 0.91) — the strongest resonance point in the audience's psychological landscape. The lead type leverages the Technique Deflection latent emotion (ELG-01, magnitude 0.85) which is the highest-magnitude expressed-vs-latent gap identified in research.
lead_type.vault_precedent: Enemy reveal leads appear in 23% of highest-converting vault controls for Stage 4 markets. They work when the 'enemy' is a hidden system/paradigm rather than a person — which matches the rearward CG villain framing.

alternatives.rationale: Opens with the Face Down Balance demo — the single strongest proof element. Shows rather than tells. Immediately creates visual intrigue that drives scroll-through.
alternatives.trade_off: Gains: immediate visual engagement, proof-before-claim structure is credible at Stage 4. Loses: the emotional vindication that makes the demo MEANINGFUL. Without the root cause context, the FDB demo is interesting but not emotionally charged.

alternatives.rationale: Opens with Chris McGinley's credentials and the question: 'What if the world's most experienced putter designer told you every putter — including the ones he built — had the same hidden flaw?'
alternatives.trade_off: Gains: immediate credibility, leverages the strongest authority asset. Loses: slower emotional engagement — the golfer must care about the engineer before caring about the revelation. Better for low-handicap audience segment.

anti_recommendations.why_not: Most Aware lead type. RS1 audience is Solution Aware (Level 3), not Most Aware. Leading with the offer before establishing root cause and mechanism would feel like an ad, not a discovery.
Violates Soul.md voice register (quiet power, earned authority). 'You won't believe what this putter does' energy is the anti-voice. The product earns attention through physics, not through teasing.

lead_type.human_rationale: Self-approved per overnight protocol. Vindication Lead is the natural expression of The Bent Arrow Big Idea as a page opener. The root cause revelation creates the emotional context that makes every subsequent element — mechanism, proof, promise, offer — land with maximum impact.

story_type.rationale: The RS1 campaign's strongest proof assets are demonstrable (FDB) and authority-based (McGinley). A Revelation Story naturally showcases both: McGinley discovers/confirms the CG problem across decades of putter design, develops Forward Weighting to solve it, and the FDB demo serves as the story's climax — the moment the physics become visible. This matches the evolutionary narrative framework (old putters → LAB → RS1) established in the Big Idea output.

alternatives.rationale: McGinley's 25-year journey from major OEMs to designing the RS1 — the first putter that puts the weight where it should be. Why did it take 25 years? Because extreme forward weighting requires 10-piece multi-material construction that wasn't practical before.
alternatives.trade_off: Gains: deep credibility through the journey narrative. Loses: pacing — the origin story is longer to develop and may lose the serious amateur audience segment before reaching the physics.

alternatives.rationale: The FDB demo as a live experiment: 'Take any putter. Any brand. Any price. Place it face-down on a flat surface. Watch what happens. Now do the same with this one.'
alternatives.trade_off: Gains: maximum proof impact, creates immediate differentiation. Loses: the emotional and historical context that explains WHY the demo matters. Best used as a supplementary content piece (video ad, social content) rather than primary story.

anti_recommendations.why_not: No genuine vulnerability to share — Performance Golf is the brand, not a person who overcame putting struggles. A manufactured vulnerability narrative would violate Soul.md (anti-voice: hallucinated scenarios).

Fear-based framing violates Soul.md voice register. 'If you don't buy this, you'll keep missing putts' is infomercial energy. The RS1 campaign earns attention through physics and vindication, not through fear.

story_type.human_rationale: Self-approved per overnight protocol. The Revelation Story is the natural container for all RS1 proof assets (McGinley credentials, FDB demo, physics explanation) and mirrors the Big Idea narrative (a hidden truth revealed).

emotional_arc.opening_emotion: Recognition — the missed 4-footer for par, the tighter hands, the ball sliding right. The golfer sees their own experience described with precision. This creates the 'this is for me' response.
emotional_arc.lead_transition: Recognition → Surprise/Curiosity — 'That miss wasn't mental. It was mechanical.' The reframe creates a pattern interrupt. The golfer's internal narrative shifts from self-blame to physics-curiosity.
emotional_arc.story_emotion: Vindication — the revelation that every putter shared the same flaw. The golfer's history is rewritten: the carousel wasn't weakness, the missed putts weren't mental, the technique was never the problem. Relief and vindication flood in.
emotional_arc.argument_emotion: Understanding → Confidence — the mechanism explanation (Forward Weighting) provides intellectual satisfaction. The three-layer explanation meets each audience where they are. The hammer analogy makes the physics intuitive. The FDB demo makes them tangible.
emotional_arc.reveal_emotion: Desire — the product is introduced as the embodiment of the solution they now understand and believe in. RS1 IS the straightened arrow. The engineering details (10-piece construction, McGinley's design) build confidence in the solution.
emotional_arc.offer_emotion: Confidence + Urgency — $399-429 undercuts both premium competitors. The guarantee removes risk. The Founders 500 creates legitimate urgency. The golfer feels confident in the value and motivated by the limited availability.
emotional_arc.close_emotion: Resolution — 'Move the weight forward. The fight is over. Let it fall.' The emotional arc resolves from frustration → curiosity → vindication → understanding → confidence → resolution. The golfer is ready to act because every emotional need has been addressed.

tone_direction.secondary_tone: Empathetic directness — the golfer is spoken to as a peer, not a customer
avoid: Infomercial hype ('game-changing,' 'never before seen,' 'you won't believe')
Generic golf marketing ('take your game to the next level')
Dumbed-down science (patronizing explanations that insult the audience's intelligence)
Adversarial competitor bashing (evolutionary narrative, not adversarial positioning)
Hallucinated specs or fabricated claims (proof ceiling respected throughout)
Forced urgency (countdown timers, fake scarcity, pressure tactics)
tone_direction.calibration_notes: Soul.md defines the voice as 'world-class engineer and sharp creative director co-presenting to golfers who know golf. Not dumbing anything down. Not hyping anything up.' Energy signature: quiet power + earned authority. Pacing: short declarative statements, one idea per line, technical fact → stroke impact → game impact. The tone must feel like a premium product presentation — Apple keynote energy, not QVC energy.

section_briefs.section_purpose: Capture attention and create the emotional entry point (vindication) that makes the rest of the page feel inevitable. Open with the 4-footer miss scenario, deliver the reframe ('That miss wasn't mental. It was mechanical.'), and create the information gap that drives scroll-through.
coherence_requirements: Lead must reference the root cause without explaining the mechanism yet
Anchor phrase 'That miss wasn't mental. It was mechanical.' must appear
The 'bent arrow' metaphor should be planted as a seed, not fully explained
constraints: No product introduction in the lead
No price, no SKUs, no offer elements
No 'game-changing' or hype language

section_briefs.section_purpose: Reveal the root cause (rearward CG creates face drift) and explain why every previous solution failed (all putters shared the same flaw). Create the 'aha' moment that makes the mechanism feel inevitable.
key_assets: Now/After grid — doing dimension (steering, carousel behavior)
coherence_requirements: Root cause must use the physics language from root-cause-package.yaml
Must explain WHY nothing worked (not just WHAT didn't work)
Must set up the 'what if the weight moved forward?' question
constraints: No mechanism explanation yet — only the root cause revelation
No specific scoring claims
Stay within proof ceiling

section_briefs.section_purpose: Explain Forward Weighting using the three-layer approach (simple → medium → technical). Deploy the hammer analogy. Introduce the FDB demo as visual proof. Establish the dual promise (technology + technique assist).
coherence_requirements: Must connect directly to the root cause: 'If the weight is behind the shaft → what happens when you move it forward?'
Must deploy FDB demo as the climax of the mechanism section
Must introduce the dual promise distinction (technology + technique assist)
constraints: Do not overclaim into distance control
Use physics language, not marketing language
Respect Soul.md voice: quiet power, earned authority

section_briefs.section_purpose: Stack proof elements: McGinley credentials, 75%/25% spec, 10-piece construction, FDB repeatability, physics principles. Eliminate remaining skepticism. Address the 'just more marketing?' objection.
coherence_requirements: All proof elements must trace to upstream packages
Must include at least one 'reason why' proof type
Must address Stage 4 mechanism fatigue skepticism directly
constraints: No fabricated test results
No 'best putter ever' superlatives without substantiation
Evolutionary narrative for competitor comparison (not adversarial)

section_briefs.section_purpose: Introduce the RS1 as a product (specs, construction, design details). Position against LAB Golf and Scotty Cameron using the evolutionary narrative (Gen 1 → Gen 2 → Gen 3). Address pressure performance.
coherence_requirements: Comparison must be evolutionary, not adversarial
Price comparison naturally leads into the offer section
Pressure performance section resolves the deepest emotional pain (short putt anxiety)
constraints: Respectful of LAB Golf
No adversarial competitor bashing
Scotty Cameron positioned as craftsmanship without mechanism, not as inferior

section_briefs.section_purpose: Present the two SKUs ($399/$429), Founders 500 edition, guarantee, and price anchoring. Execute the value demonstration ('if all it did was...'). Create the buying decision.
coherence_requirements: Price anchoring must reference Scotty Cameron and LAB Golf prices from earlier comparison
Guarantee language must match offer-package.json exactly
Founders 500 scarcity must be genuine, not forced
constraints: No inflated value stacking
No fake scarcity or countdown timers
Premium branded eCom tone, not DR infomercial energy

section_briefs.section_purpose: Deliver the emotional resolution. Consequence amplification (what happens if they don't act). Final CTA with Founders 500 urgency. End on feeling, not logistics.
key_assets: Campaign close lines ('Move the weight forward. The fight is over.')
coherence_requirements: Close must echo the opening anchor phrase ('That miss wasn't mental. It was mechanical.') to create narrative closure
Tagline 'Let it fall.' appears as the final emotional beat
The identity resolution ('complete golfer') resolves the identity tension planted in the lead
constraints: End on emotion, not logistics
No new information — the close uses only what has been established
Final line is feeling, not feature

open_loops.loop_description: The Bent Arrow reference — 'the arrow has been bent this whole time' is planted in the lead as an unexplained metaphor
open_loops.resolved_in: Mechanism section (Forward Weighting = the straightened arrow) and Close ('the arrow is finally straight')
open_loops.purpose: Creates curiosity about the metaphor's meaning while providing a memorable image that the golfer carries through the entire page

open_loops.loop_description: The Face Down Balance test — 'place any putter face-down on a surface, watch what happens' is teased but not fully explained in the lead/root cause section
open_loops.planted_in: Root cause section (brief mention of a test that proves the weight position)
open_loops.resolved_in: Mechanism/proof section (full FDB demo explanation with comparison images)
open_loops.purpose: Creates a specific curiosity gap — the golfer wants to see this test — that drives scroll-through to the mechanism section

open_loops.loop_description: The dual promise tease — 'there's a putter that does something no other putter does: two things at once' is planted without explanation
open_loops.planted_in: Root cause section (after explaining that LAB solved one problem but not both)
open_loops.resolved_in: Mechanism section (Forward Weighting eliminates face drift AND assists technique — the dual promise reveal)
open_loops.purpose: Creates curiosity about what 'two things at once' means, differentiating RS1 from the single-pillar positioning of every competitor

open_loops.loop_description: The designer identity — 'designed by someone who built clubs for 11 world #1 players' is mentioned without naming McGinley
open_loops.purpose: Creates authority curiosity — who is this person? — that drives engagement with the credibility section

open_loops.loop_description: The carousel cost — 'how much have you spent on putters that all shared the same design flaw?' is posed without answering
open_loops.resolved_in: Offer section (price anchoring includes carousel cost of $1,000-3,000+ as comparison)
open_loops.purpose: Plants the price reframe early so the $399 reveal feels like a steal compared to cumulative carousel spending

recurring_elements.element: 'That miss wasn't mental. It was mechanical.' — the primary anchor phrase
recurrences: Pressure performance section (revisited under pressure context)
recurring_elements.purpose: The anchor phrase is the emotional spine of the campaign. Each recurrence adds a new layer of meaning: first as a surprise, then as an explanation, then as a comfort under pressure, then as a final resolution.

recurring_elements.first_appearance: End of lead section (after the reframe)
recurrences: Mechanism section (as technique instruction: let the heavy end fall forward)
Close section (as the final emotional beat — both let gravity work AND let the ball fall in)
recurring_elements.purpose: The tagline carries three meanings that accumulate: (1) let gravity do the work, (2) let the ball fall in the hole, (3) let go of the fight. Each recurrence activates a new layer.

recurring_elements.element: The hammer analogy — heavy end forward, you don't steer it
recurring_elements.first_appearance: Mechanism section (primary analogy for Forward Weighting)
recurrences: Proof section (extended: 'imagine swinging a hammer backwards — that's what every putter has been doing')
Value section (compressed callback: 'heavy end forward — the way every tool should work')
recurring_elements.purpose: The hammer is the persistent image anchor that makes Forward Weighting intuitive. Once the golfer sees the hammer, they can't un-see it — and every putter they pick up afterward gets evaluated against the analogy.

transition_rules.guidance: The hero headline + image should create immediate visual engagement. The lead begins directly below with the 4-footer scenario. No explicit transition needed — the scroll IS the transition.
The lead plants the reframe. The root cause section escalates: 'And it goes deeper than one missed putt.' Expand from a single miss to the pattern of misses across every putter the golfer has owned.

This is the pivotal transition. The root cause section ends with the question: 'What happens when you move the weight forward?' The mechanism section IS the answer. Use 'And that's exactly what RS1 does' as the coherence marker.

Pivot from explanation to evidence: 'That's the engineering. But how do you know it's real?' This acknowledges the Stage 4 audience's skepticism and frames the proof section as the answer to their doubt.
Escalate from 'the physics work' to 'the physics work when it matters most.' The pressure performance section is the emotional climax of the education portion.

The comparison section naturally includes price points for Scotty and LAB. Bridge to the offer: 'Gen 3 putter design. A physics advantage neither competitor can claim. And the price might surprise you.'
Callback to the opening: 'The weight has been in the wrong place your entire putting life.' Then resolve: 'Move it forward. The fight is over. Let it fall.'

choices_made.rationale: Strongest alignment with Big Idea (The Bent Arrow), FSSIT-01 (impact 0.91), and market psychology (Technique Deflection, ELG-01)
Leverages strongest proof assets (FDB demo, McGinley credentials) within a narrative that mirrors the Big Idea discovery structure
Recognition → surprise → vindication → understanding → confidence → desire → resolution is the natural emotional progression of The Bent Arrow revelation
Scientific authority with restrained confidence matches Soul.md voice register (quiet power + earned authority)
5 open loops and 3 recurring elements provide sufficient narrative continuity for premium eCom page format

human_decisions.approval_note: Self-approved per overnight execution protocol. User sleeping. All decision points documented with rationale. No overrides required — all recommendations aligned with upstream strategy.
human_decisions.execution_notes: All downstream writing skills should load this brief first. The Soul.md voice register (quiet power + earned authority) is non-negotiable. The proof ceiling (significant improvement, no fabricated outcome data) applies to all sections. The Bent Arrow is the Big Idea — every section should serve this metaphor. 'Let it fall.' is the tagline — use sparingly for maximum impact (lead, mechanism, close only).
